response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong software craftsmanship background, but his profile has essentially no overlap with the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerator hardware. The role demands SoC/IP modeling, deep-learning workload analysis, hardware-software co-design, and GPU/SIMD architecture expertise — none of which appear in his resume. His closest hardware-adjacent work is a bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis and some low-level C/C++ at National Instruments, both too peripheral and dated to be relevant. His performance work is limited to application-level latency optimization in web services, not silicon or accelerator-level performance modeling.
strengths: Long career spanning 15+ years with demonstrated depth in low-latency, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O in DLang)
Broad technical range including embedded systems, C/C++, hardware tooling at National Instruments, and robotic arm development at Altom
BS in Computer Science from IIT Bombay with 14+ years of experience, formally meeting the education/experience threshold
concerns: No experience with SoC or IP modeling, AI accelerators, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD workloads — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep neural network training, inference optimization, or any ML/AI systems work beyond general AI-agent tooling at a product level
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack development; hardware-software co-design and cloud infrastructure for chip design are entirely outside his demonstrated domain

key_factors.factor: No AI/ML workload or GPU architecture knowledge
Low-latency systems and some hardware exposure
